content.full_text:
EPPC
5 924 W City of Woodland

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY          CITY HALL          WOODLAND, CALIFORNIA 95695          (916) [illegible]
                                                                                              661-5307

                                        September 4, 1990


John Rothman
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento CA  95804-0807

Dear Mr. Rothman:

     Woodland Council Members Elaine Timothy and Joe Crescione have
asked me to request a legal opinion from the FPPC regarding the
application of the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act.  Specifically, Council Members Timothy and
Crescione both own real property located within 2500 feet from a
proposed development project, the Taormino Project.  Accordingly,
we would like an opinion as to as to whether this fact precludes
them from participating in Woodland City Council decisions
regarding this project.

     In order to assist you in your analysis, I have compiled the
following facts.  The Taormino Project is still in the early
stages of development, however, the developer has proposed 260
single family homes to be situated on lots ranging from 14,500
to 20,000 square feet.  The developer also anticipates
significant front and side-yard setbacks.  The Taormino Project
will also have a small lake with a park and a gazebo as well as
substantial amounts of open space for recreational uses such as
playgrounds, jogging and strolling.  Finally, the project will
have covenants, conditions and restrictions "CC & R's" to uphold
design standards which will be monitored by an architectural
review committee.

     Council Members Joe Crecione and Elaine Timothy own homes located
at 1614 Donner Way and 703 Rubicon Court, respectively in the
same neighborhood in Woodland.  Enclosed are opinions from two
local Real Estate firms regarding prices of houses in their
neighborhood as well as recent housing market information for the
Woodland area.

     In summary, we request a legal opinion on whether the Taormino
Project will have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect of
$10,000.00 or more on the fair market value of the real


                                        City of Trees

John Rotham
Page 2
August 31, 1990

property owned by Council members Timothy and Crecione.
Furthermore, we request a legal opinion on whether any material
financial effects, if found or even if only a potential conflict
is found, will be distinguishable from its effects on the public
generally.

Presently, the Woodland City Council has placed the project on
hold until this conflict issue has been resolved. While we
realize the FPPC has 21 days to respond, we respectfully request
a response as soon as possible.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.
Should you have any questions, please feel free to call this
office.

Very truly yours,

RONALD E. MOE

REM/kla

Enclosure

LYNCH & ASSOCIATES
real estate appraisals & consultation

21 court street
woodland, CA 95695

bus. 916 - 666-6626

Aug. 27, 1990

Janet Ruggiero, Community Dev. Director
City of Woodland
300 First St.
Woodland, Ca 95695

RE: Donner Way, Rubicon Pl. Neighborhood

Dear Janet Ruggiero,

     The proposed development on the south side of Woodland will
have no local affect on the above referenced neighborhood, but
will have an overall affect on all similar priced homes in
Woodland.  A greater supply of a given value of home will lower
the demand and lessen the upward pressure on market value.

     The average price home in the above referenced neighborhood
increased 45% from May 1989 to May 1990. The range is from 25.3%
to 59.1%. The increase from May 1990 to present, based on current
listings, is about 10% for four (4) months.

     If I can be of further help do not hesitate to call me.

Respectfully submitted,

John C. Lynch

JCL:gm

1520 s.f.  4/2            $126,600    5-08-89    $83.29/s.f.
all new kitchen

enclosed patio and pool

1600 s.f.  3/2            $123,500    5-02-89    $77.19/s.f.

1654 Donner Way
1645 s.f.  4/2            $116,000    5-08-89    $70.52/s.f.


1990 SALES

716 El Dorado
1514 s.f.  4/2            $158,000    4-24-90    $104.36/s.f.

1544 Truckee Way
1649 s.f.  3/2            $185,000    7-31-90    $112.19/s.f.
pool and redone kitchen

718 Placer
1521 s.f.  4/2            $162,500    7-17-90    $106.83/s.f.
inground spa

1719 Quincy
1400 s.f.  4/2            $155,000    8-10-90    $110.71/s.f.


LISTINGS

809 Sonora Pl.
1516 s.f.  3/2            $185,500                $122.36/s.f.

224 W. Gibson
1501 s.f.  3/2.5          $185,000                $123.25/s.f.

437 Truckee
1400 s.f.  3/2            $175,000                $125/s.f.


1989 sales range         $70.52 to $83.29/s.f.
1990 sales range         $104.36 to $112.19/s.f.
Current listings         $122.36 to $125.00/s.f.

sections.question: Whether the Taormino Project will have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect of $10,000.00 or more on the fair market value of the real property owned by Council members Timothy and Crescione, and whether any material financial effects would be distinguishable from its effects on the public generally.
sections.question_synthetic: Two Woodland City Council members own residential property within 2,500 feet of a proposed 260-unit residential development (Taormino Project). The City Attorney requests a legal opinion on whether this proximity triggers conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically whether the project would have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect of $10,000 or more on the council members' property values.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal Q/A or conclusion sections present. Real estate appraisals and sales data included as supporting attachments. Letter requests expedited response despite 21-day standard timeline. Minor date inconsistency (letter header says Sept 4, 1990 but signed August 31, 1990).

embedding.summary: City of Woodland requests an FPPC legal opinion regarding conflict of interest issues for two council members who own property near a proposed residential development, seeking guidance on whether the development would materially affect their property values and disqualify them from voting on the project.
